channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Meet the 2024 candidates for the 35th Assembly District: Jasmeet Bains
item.description: Assemblywoman Dr. Jasmeet Bains is a lawmaker of many firsts, but she was once the political newcomer.
item.dc:creator: Jenny Huh, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump edges out Harris with early voters in battleground states
item.description: Former President Trump has a slight edge over Vice President Harris with early voters in battleground states, new polling shows, a promising sign for the Republican in the highly competitive presidential race.
item.dc:creator: Julia Mueller, NewsNation

item.title: The Latest: Trump and Harris make appearances in Pennsylvania, the largest battleground state
item.description: Democrat Kamala Harris and Republican Donald Trump took their presidential campaigns back to Pennsylvania on Monday. About 7 million votes are up for grabs in the election's largest battleground state, where mail-in voting is well underway.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Kamala Harris accused of plagiarizing in 2009 book about being 'smart on crime'
item.description: Harris is accused of plagiarizing several times in her book on smart policing, which was released in 2009.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hurricane-hit battleground states face additional voting hurdles in run-up to Election Day
item.description: Voters in storm-ravaged parts of North Carolina and other Southern states could have limited access to the polls this November.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump suggests using National Guard, military against 'enemy from within'
item.description: Former President Donald Trump in a Sunday interview suggested using the National Guard or the military on Election Day.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, NewsNation

item.title: One-on-one with Mississippi's candidates in 2024 US Senate race
item.description: JACKSON, Miss. (WJTV) - U.S. Senator Roger Wicker (R-Miss.) and his Democratic opponent, Ty Pinkins, spoke to WJTV 12 News ahead of the November General Election. Wicker, a Pontotoc native, has been serving Mississippi in the U.S. Senate since 2007.
item.dc:creator: Tom Wright, NewsNation

item.title: Kamala Harris will sit down with Bret Baier for her first Fox News interview
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Vice President Kamala Harris will be interviewed by Fox News anchor Bret Baier on Wednesday in Pennsylvania as she steps up her travel and conversations with media outlets in the closing stretch of the presidential campaign.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Sending a THAAD air defense system to Israel adds to strain on US Army forces
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The deployment of a U.S. Terminal High Altitude Area Defense battery to Israel and roughly 100 soldiers to operate it will add to already difficult strains on the Army's air defense forces and potential delays in modernizing its missile defense systems, Army leaders said Monday.
item.dc:creator: LOLITA C. BALDOR, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Harris doubles down with 'Indigenous Peoples' Day' post amid outrage over Columbus Day rhetoric
item.description: Amid outrage over Vice President Kamala Harris' previous rhetoric on ending the recognition of Columbus Day, Harris doubled down with an X post celebrating Indigenous Peoples’ Day.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: NJ lawmakers' fight against teen vaping continues with new bill
item.description: TRENTON, N.J. (PIX11) -- The fight against youth vaping continues in the Garden State as some Trenton lawmakers are trying to pass a law prohibiting the delivery of electronic smoking devices to people under 21, requiring someone over 21 to sign off on the delivery.
item.dc:creator: Jim Vasil, PIX 11

item.title: Fox News Politics: Who is to blame for the border?
item.description: The latest updates from the 2024 campaign trail, exclusive interviews and more Fox News politics content
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Afghan charged with Election Day terror plot raises questions, fears from lawmakers: ‘This is real’
item.description: An Election Day terror plot, allegedly being planned by an Afghan national who came to the U.S. in 2021, is raising new concerns about the numbers brought to the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 3 voter-related lawsuits dropped in Nevada ahead of Election Day
item.description: Petitioners who filed three lawsuits challenging the maintenance of Nevada’s voter rolls have dismissed their cases, in part because voting by-mail ballot was already well underway.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Miriam Adelson's $1 million donation to Gov. Kemp's PAC could help Trump in Georgia
item.description: Republican megadonor Miriam Adelson has contributed $1 million to Georgia Gov. Brian Kemp's political action committee.
item.dc:creator: Thomas Beaumont, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Democrat Hakeem Jeffries stumps in California in a bid to reclaim House control
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Democratic Leader Hakeem Jeffries crisscrossed Southern California over the weekend in communities far from the presidential battlegrounds — Little Saigon in Orange County and the high desert of Antelope Valley — as he works to wrest control of the U.S. House from Republicans.
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Washington state's landmark climate law hangs in the balance this election
item.description: SEATTLE (AP) — A groundbreaking law that forces companies in Washington state to reduce their carbon emissions while raising billions of dollars for climate programs could be repealed by voters this fall, less than two years after it took effect.
item.dc:creator: HALLIE GOLDEN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Things to know about AP's report on the federal criminal cases against Donald Trump
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A year that began with the prospect of a federal court reckoning for Donald Trump will conclude without any chance at trial, leaving voters without the finality of an up-or-down jury verdict in the two most consequential cases against the Republican presidential nominee.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER and ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump suggests using National Guard, military against 'enemy from within'
item.description: Former President Donald Trump in a Sunday interview suggested using the National Guard or the military on Election Day to combat what he described as potential chaos from “the enemy from within” – a group that Trump said includes “radical left lunatics.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Harris and Trump hold rival events in battleground Pennsylvania
item.description: Vice President Kamala Harris and former President Donald Trump have taken their fight for Pennsylvania’s Electoral College votes to opposite ends of the state.
item.dc:creator: Mark Scolforo, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Harris gives Democrats a boost, but control of House up for grabs
item.description: Democrats have a fair shot at flipping control of the House — largely because of the boost provided by Vice President Harris at the top of the ticket — but the battle is a horse race that is too close to call heading into the final weeks of the campaign, according to a host of
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Senate rankings: 5 seats most likely to flip
item.description: The battle for the Senate majority has hit its final month as Republicans race to end their four-year stint in the minority. The GOP needs to win only one of two competitive races in red states — Montana and Ohio — to do just that, barring a surprise loss elsewhere.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, Fox 5

item.title: Bob Woodward takes NPR behind the headline-grabbing moments in his new book
item.description: Bob Woodward speaks to NPR about the revelations in his new book, and recounts how key moments and meetings in recent years played out behind closed doors.
item.dc:creator: Scott Detrow, Courtney Dorning, and Avery Keatley, MPR News

item.title: Indigenous Peoples Day celebrated with an eye on the election
item.description: Many Native Americans across the U.S. are coming together for Indigenous Peoples Day to celebrate their history and culture and acknowledge the ongoing challenges they face with a focus on the election.
item.dc:creator: Hallie Golden, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Riverside Co. sheriff says deputies 'probably' prevented third assassination attempt on Trump
item.description: A man was arrested outside of a campaign rally for former President Donald Trump in Southern California, and multiple guns were illegally in his possession, according to authorities. The arrest took place at a checkpoint at the intersection of Avenue 52 and Celebration Drive in Coachella at 4:59 p.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, NewsNation

item.title: Trump tells protester to 'go back home to Mommy' during rally
item.description: Trump called for the protester to “get the hell knocked out of her,” his latest instance of using violent language when confronted by demonstrators.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Walz defends using the term 'socialism': 'It's what the right uses'
item.description: Minnesota Gov. Tim Walz (D) on Sunday defended his use of the word "socialism" over the summer, saying he believes "capitalism works" but that there are things society must do collectively.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Scully, NewsNation

item.title: Donald Trump holds a rally in California, a state he's almost certain to lose
item.description: The White House election is expected to come down to battlegrounds such as Wisconsin and Pennsylvania.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Trump hits Harris on immigration in her home state of California
item.description: Former President Trump slammed Vice President Harris in her native California on Saturday evening, ranting about migrants and bashing illegal immigration in the Golden State.
item.dc:creator: Hanna Trudo, NewsNation

item.title: No Dem effort to stymie Trump election win certification: Strategists
item.description: A few Democratic lawmakers say they will accept a free and fair Trump election win, but aren't sure he'll be fair or honest.
item.dc:creator: Rich Johnson, NewsNation

item.title: Henderson City Council candidate Dr. Monica Larson discusses government transparency, public safety, and housing
item.description: Henderson City Council candidate Dr. Monica Larson joined the 8 News Now Daily Voting Guide Friday to discuss issues she believes need to be addressed in the city.
item.dc:creator: Justin Walker, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Battle to shore up bases remains as Election Day nears
item.description: MPR News politics editor Brian Bakst and his guest talks about deadly conflicts in the Middle East and how those have factored into domestic politics. Plus, we follow up with two Generation Z voters about how they’re approaching the election.
item.dc:creator: Brian Bakst, Matthew Alvarez, and Ellie Roth, MPR News
